sections.markdown:
- 8 km easy aerobic run
- Keep this fully conversational after Tuesday's shorter hill-repeat session.
- Start the first 10 to 15 minutes very controlled, then settle into relaxed aerobic running without turning it into a steady day.
- Tuesday still counted as quality because Monday had already come in harder than planned, so do not use this run to reclaim any missed threshold volume.
- Let the route be rolling if you want, but flatten the effort on climbs so heart rate stays supportive rather than drifting toward threshold again.
- If Tuesday leaves more residual fatigue than expected, shorten to `6 to 7 km` instead of forcing the full `8 km`.

- This stayed on the planned Wednesday date and mostly stayed in the intended aerobic-support role. The run covered `8.381 km` in `49:29` moving time (`50:09` elapsed) with `100.8 m` of climbing, so total volume landed only `0.381 km` over the planned `8 km`.
- The start matched the prescription well enough. The first `2.00 km` averaged about `5:41 /km` at average HR `146.2` despite `35.8 m` of climbing, which is controlled enough to count as the patient opening the note asked for.
- The run got costlier later. The second half averaged HR `158.3` at about `5:58 /km`, compared with HR `148.7` at about `5:47 /km` in the first half, and the final `2.381 km` averaged HR `160.5` with another `43.0 m` of climbing. That is still materially easier than Tuesday's average HR `165.2`, but it shows the session stopped being fully conversational by the end, which fits the mostly clear `23.4 C` average temperature and `25.2 C` apparent conditions.

- The useful win here is that Wednesday returned the week to real aerobic running after Monday's `12.194 km` drift and Tuesday's hill-repeat substitute. The week now sits at `27.6 km` across four runs, and this was the first run of the week to bring average HR down instead of pushing it back into the mid-160s.
- The next few days should treat that as enough. Thursday's `10 km` steady run should stay genuinely steady rather than turning into make-up threshold work, because the late-run HR drift today says the aerobic cost was already rising even while pace slowed.
- Friday's recovery run and Sunday's long run will benefit more from absorbing this session than from squeezing extra pace out of Thursday in similar heat.

- This run supports the block because it preserved frequency and kept the planned aerobic day on schedule after two more expensive days. Getting to `27.6 km` by Wednesday inside a planned `63 km` week is useful only if the easier sessions still look measurably easier than the quality work.
- The caution remains effort separation. Monday averaged HR `164.6`, Tuesday averaged HR `165.2`, and Wednesday came down to `153.7`, but the second-half drift and slowing pace show that easy-day control is still one of the main skills this block needs.
- For the half-marathon build and the marathon goal, the long-term gain is not just handling more weekly volume. It is handling that volume while keeping aerobic days aerobic enough that threshold, steady, and long-run work stay repeatable.
- tried to keep my HR down low, but sun was out and shining.

- Apple Health recorded `242` during-run heart-rate samples. Average HR was `153.8 bpm`, ranging from `80.0` to `172.3 bpm`.
- First-half average HR was `147.9 bpm` and second-half average HR was `158.5 bpm` (+10.6 bpm), which shows clear late cardiovascular drift.

- Apple Health cadence averaged `172.0 spm`, with a working range of `116.0` to `212.5 spm` after excluding `2` start-stop outliers below `100 spm`.
- First-half cadence averaged `172.0 spm` and second-half cadence averaged `171.9 spm` (-0.1 spm), which looks mechanically stable.

- Strava moving pace averaged `5:54 /km` across `8.381 km` in `49:29` moving time.
- Estimated first-half moving pace was `5:49 /km` and second-half pace was `5:59 /km` (+10 s/km), so pace eased a little late.

- Strava summary HR came in at `153.7 bpm` average and `173 bpm` max.
- Distance-split HR averaged `148.6 bpm` in the first half and `158.3 bpm` in the second (+9.7 bpm), which shows clear late cardiovascular drift.

- Moving time was `49:29` out of `50:09` elapsed (98.7%), which kept stoppage cost negligible.
- The downsampled moving stream shows `6` stop segments and `0.6%` stationary samples, so the run was mostly continuous with a few interruptions.

- Strava logged about `104.4 m` of climbing, with altitude moving between roughly `42.2 m` and `66.0 m`.
- Estimated climbing was `49.6 m` in the first half and `54.8 m` in the second, so the climbing load was split fairly evenly.
